evidence: V: 'So the demo is like human data. Like it's a human data oriented demo.'

Logan: 'Can we make like a 30 second look at the information that we capture via Careerspan? Because if that's in an email... they don't have to wrap their heads around what it is we're describing. It's just like oh shit, I want that information.'
Logan: 'Boom, mic drop for the hiring people. This is opted in candidate data... as things heat up with publicly scraped data, this is a really valuable strategy for you guys.'
Reference to Eightfold lawsuit — positioning against publicly scraped data risks
Logan: 'I'm thinking a deck is not as impactful as watching it in action.'
Logan: 'It's like kind of a baller move to put the lead list of workforce boards in. That's kind of like just giving value up front.'
Logan: 'The problem with our existing materials is that it takes a fuck ton of word space to explain what it is that we do and why it's relevant.'
Positioning crystallized — Careerspan sells human data, not software features. Shift from 'enrichment layer' framing to 'human data company'
V: 'You want Spiegel as a bot, right? Is you. How much of it do you think is just the psychological reassurance that when they're getting advice it's through the lens that you have.'
David confirms the core ambition - building a system that queries his knowledge base and provides advice in his voice/perspective

source_display: Messaging Bot as Primary Pain Point
evidence: David: 'Where I see people end up needing the most help after they learn from me, is getting stuck on what to message. And it's not just what to initially message for cold outreach, it's like what to message for warm outreach first.'

David: 'I think we need to help Zo articulate that. I can't explain what Zo is because compared to other AIs. Other than to say it allows you to store multiple files and it's like its own instance of files and you can use any model.'
V: 'deepwiki.com you can add any repository and it will turn it into a Wikipedia. That's pretty cool. It explains much more clearly what all is possible in N5OS.'
David immediately recognized DeepWiki as a way to understand N5OS architecture without technical knowledge - 'Oh, cool. I'm looking at it now. That's cool.'
Decision to start David with simpler meeting processing system rather than complex messaging system - focus on achievable project first
V: 'You could create a private GitHub repo with all of your notes from your different sessions and actually store them in a way that your AI can access.'
V: 'At the end of every conversation where I build something or I like do something sort of like major, I will essentially press close conversation... have it like tidy up after itself.'
David identified the ICP as people 'just smart enough' who have hit limitations with ChatGPT projects/custom GPTs - the frustrated power user segment
David: 'I owe an email to Ben Erez. I want to get a couple emails to him, but the first one I want is to introduce him to the three of you.'

V: 'I'm actually hoping to plug them up into my Zoe so that... There's a character in Sherlock that had these glasses that told him everything. Like, gave him the superpower of total knowledge or omniscience. So that's almost what I want to emulate.'
V: 'Throw in an emoji at the start of the sentence, like a pause button... that's really important to develop that discipline, because otherwise you'll get absolutely inundated in threads.'
